% auto-generated by scripts/p27_seed_inference.py -- do not hand-edit
% The seed-level analysis that matters when the deployable artifact is ONE
% training run, not the mean of three. Outer bootstrap over the three seeds, inner
% over the hundred instances, both sides of every comparison pooled symmetrically.
% n=3 cannot certify a seed-level claim; these macros exist so the paper can say so
% with a number instead of a shrug.
\newcommand{\SeedN}{3}  % independent training runs behind every E1/E2 verdict
\newcommand{\SeedEqInstance}{4}\newcommand{\SeedEqSeedLevel}{3}  % E1 regimes EQUIVALENT at +-1%: instance-level, then seed-level
\newcommand{\SeedWidenX}{2.3}  % how much wider the seed-level interval is than the instance-level one, worst regime
\newcommand{\SdNOne}{+0.55}\newcommand{\SdNTwo}{-0.25}\newcommand{\SdNThree}{+0.08}\newcommand{\SdNLo}{-0.44}\newcommand{\SdNHi}{+0.77}  % N: the three per-seed gaps, then the seed-level interval (%)
\newcommand{\VdNInstHalf}{\textsc{eq}}  % N, inst-level verdict at +-0.5%
\newcommand{\VdNInstOne}{\textsc{eq}}  % N, inst-level verdict at +-1.0%
\newcommand{\VdNInstTwo}{\textsc{eq}}  % N, inst-level verdict at +-2.0%
\newcommand{\VdNSeedHalf}{\textsc{inc}}  % N, seed-level verdict at +-0.5%
\newcommand{\VdNSeedOne}{\textsc{eq}}  % N, seed-level verdict at +-1.0%
\newcommand{\VdNSeedTwo}{\textsc{eq}}  % N, seed-level verdict at +-2.0%
